claims.claim: config-conventional configures no scope-enum/scope-case/scope-empty rule, so any scope passes the gate
claims.evidence: Consistent with the other three runs, which independently read node_modules/@commitlint/config-conventional/lib/index.js and reached the same conclusion; scope-vocabulary.md lists the exact enforced rule set.

claims.claim: The scope convention is written down nowhere in the repo and exists only in directory layout and commit history
claims.evidence: Corroborated across all four runs; scope-vocabulary.md mines it from 'git log -n 400' and the directory tree.

eval_feedback.overall: Strongest of the four outputs: floor declaration drives a concrete design consequence (scope-as-script), and the companion scope-vocabulary.md turns the undocumented convention into a verifiable derivation rule. Assertions are discriminating overall.

suggestions.assertion: Treats the repo's commitlint configuration as a source of truth to point to, rather than copying its scope list into the skill
suggestions.reason: commitlint has no scope list to copy (no scope-enum), so the 'not copying its scope list' clause is trivially satisfiable. The discriminating fact is that the config carries the TYPE enum + format rules and no scope constraint; consider rewording to test that the design points at the config for types/format AND recognizes scope is unconstrained there.

expectations.evidence: Opens 'This brief designs a skill, not a SKILL.md' and ends 'Do not begin the SKILL.md until Open question 1 ... has an answer'. No SKILL.md frontmatter or implementation is produced.
expectations.text: Produces a design brief or design document, not a finished SKILL.md implementation

expectations.evidence: Trigger phrasings: '"commit this," "write a commit message," ...'. Near misses that must not trigger: 'How should I split this work into commits?' and 'Rebase this / clean up my branch history', both routed to software:git.
expectations.text: Names trigger phrasings and at least one nearby request that should NOT trigger the skill

expectations.evidence: 'Declared floor: the weakest model any teammate runs in Claude Code ... plan for Haiku-class execution, not Opus', with the consequence that scope derivation becomes a script.
expectations.text: Declares the weakest model or agent the skill must work for (a floor), not just the best case

expectations.evidence: 'Type vocabulary and subject rules -> commitlint.config.js / config-conventional ... The skill must not restate the type list as its own authority; it points at the config'. Correctly notes config-conventional sets no scope-enum, so scope is derived from the live filesystem, not copied.
expectations.text: Treats the repo's commitlint configuration as a source of truth to point to, rather than copying its scope list into the skill

expectations.evidence: Checks section: staged-diff fixtures -> expected commit header with paired assertions (passes commitlint --edit; scope equals derivation), plus positive/negative trigger queries. 'Baseline every fixture with no skill first'.
expectations.text: Lists concrete checks or evals to build before or alongside the skill

expectations.evidence: Six numbered Open questions (multi-area scope rule, root-tooling scope, message-vs-commit, distribution shape, subject prescriptiveness, scope-enum enforcement), gated: 'Do not begin the SKILL.md until Open question 1 ... has an answer'.
expectations.text: Surfaces unknowns as explicit open questions instead of silently assuming answers
